verdicts.rationale: Tailors advice to the user's slow-cooker history, including beef stew and yogurt.
Uses the user's remote-work context and prior team-connection ideas with specific relevant suggestions.
Suggests dinners using the recalled basil, mint, and cherry tomatoes, which matches the preference rubric.
Does not answer the painting-inspiration question and gives a generic meta response instead.
Fails to suggest cocktails and does not use the user's mixology background or preferences.
Incorrectly says there is no relevant context instead of using the prior power-bank/battery information.
Builds directly on the user's prior turbinado sugar experimentation and compatible flavor ideas.
Recommends cookies instead of building on the user's prior lemon poppyseed cake success.
Incorporates the planned new dresser and mid-century modern style into the bedroom-layout advice.
Answers an unrelated biking question and does not address guitar selection at all.
Suggests variations on the user's homemade almond-milk creamer while keeping sugar reduction and cost in view.
Mentions cat shedding, but misses the key recalled detail about the recent deep clean stirring up dust.
Despite an irrelevant opening sentence, it gives reunion advice grounded in the user's positive high-school memories and interests.
Uses the user's storage-capacity issues and external-drive reliance to support buying the NAS now.
Recommends theme-park options matched to the user's interest in thrill rides, special events, food, and nighttime shows.
Builds on the user's healthy meal-prep habits with quinoa, roasted vegetables, and protein variations.
Centers the advice on the user's prior Denver live-music experience and Brandon Flowers memory.
Recommends documentaries aligned with the user's stated tastes from Our Planet, Free Solo, and Tiger King.
Explains the better bike performance using the remembered chain/cassette replacement and Garmin-based comparison idea.
Suggests accessories appropriate for an iPhone 13 Pro and aligned with the user's prior accessory interests.
